mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains at a near-zero level, the discount-rate channel supports higher equity valuations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is refuted if rate cuts do not coincide with positive forward equity performance.
failure_condition: If triggered observations with declining FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or the rolling 2-year Rank IC is below 0.03, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening household income growth and higher recession risk, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be strongest after a clear upward labor-market break.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points in 3 months, SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases or the average return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, the market infers broad nominal-demand strength, supporting corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if sales improve without labor support or vice versa.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not improve 20-day SPX hit rate relative to either series alone, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better real activity, which should translate into higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved earnings expectations. The signal is falsified if stronger production is followed by weaker equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in INDPRO are not associated with above-baseline SPX 60-day returns, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals a housing-demand slowdown and tighter cyclical growth expectations, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is specifically active when housing weakens faster than other macro demand indicators.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drops more than 5% below its 3-month moving average yet SPX 20-day returns remain positive in a majority of cases or the conditional mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS is not increasing, real rates become more accommodative and equities should rally over the next 5 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation surprises are not met with policy tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 3-month average while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-up/policy-flat condition does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS changes are controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals labor-market resilience, which should support earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is invalid if payroll gains are offset by an unemployment increase.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling or stable UNRATE with rising PAYEMS fails to improve 20-day SPX returns relative to the unconditional mean, or if the combined signal has no incremental predictive power beyond PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad demand contraction that should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism should be especially clear when both real output and retail spending weaken together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign remains positive across any rolling 3-year window despite the contraction signal.
